label: CEX
notes: Harvested from a live CEX (July 2026). ASP.NET WebForms, same field family as CEX-C (identical cexEntry_ ids for the details/feedback/reflection sections) but with its OWN EIGHT competency items and its OWN score-ids: items are data-rating-id 9,10,11,12,13,14,15 then 66 for Professionalism (note the jump), and scores are 5=N, 6=D, 7=S, 8=O — DIFFERENT again from DOPS (16-25 / 9,10,11,32) and CEX-C (67-72 / 28-31). Three forms, three sets: never extrapolate ISCP rating ids between forms. Rater-owned fields are included so the trainee can DRAFT the assessment for the assessor, who reviews, edits and validates it with THEIR OWN PASSWORD (never touched by us). Extraction hints forbid inventing any rating or feedback not actually stated. Reflections are auto-filled by faithful rewriting (nothing invented). Pickers (rater/hospital) are AJAX widgets: reference only in v1, user picks on-site. Dates dd/mm/yyyy.

fields.label: Assessment date
fields.extractionHints: Date the consent encounter took place. Accept spoken dates.

fields.label: Assessor (rater)
fields.extractionHints: Assessing consultant. Reference only — the user picks them in ISCP's picker.

fields.label: Hospital
fields.extractionHints: Hospital. Reference only — picked on the form.

fields.label: Brief summary of case
fields.extractionHints: One-line summary of the clinical encounter, e.g. 'Assessment of acute abdominal pain'. NO patient-identifiable details.

fields.label: Clinical setting
fields.extractionHints: Where it happened ('in clinic' -> Clinic; 'on the ward' -> Ward; ITU -> Hospital - Critical care).

fields.label: Clinical setting (other)
fields.extractionHints: Only if 'Other'.

fields.label: Emergency or elective
fields.extractionHints: Emergency or elective.

fields.label: New or follow-up
fields.extractionHints: New patient or follow-up.

fields.label: Case complexity

options.label: 2. Appropriate for the completion of early years training or early specialty training
3. Appropriate for the central period of specialty training

fields.extractionHints: Complexity 1-4. Map 'level three'/'complexity 3' to the numbered option.

fields.label: Critically ill patient
fields.extractionHints: Was the patient critically ill/unstable?

fields.label: Simulated setting
fields.extractionHints: Usually No for a real patient.

fields.label: Simulation details
fields.extractionHints: Only if simulated.

fields.label: Performed on a course
fields.extractionHints: Usually No.

fields.label: Course details
fields.extractionHints: Only if on a course.

fields.label: Focus of encounter
fields.extractionHints: Which aspects the encounter covered (several allowed): history, exam, diagnosis, explanation, consent, management.

fields.label: Rating for all competencies (unless rated individually below)
fields.extractionHints: A BLANKET rating the assessor gave covering the competencies they didn't single out — 'satisfactory for everything else', 'the rest were fine'. Set ONLY if such a general statement was actually made; it fills every item not rated individually. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: 1. History Taking
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 1 ('History Taking'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 1
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 1 ('History Taking'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 2. Physical Examination Skills
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 2 ('Physical Examination Skills'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 2
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 2 ('Physical Examination Skills'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 3. Diagnostic skills and underlying knowledge base
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 3 ('Diagnostic skills and underlying knowledge base'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 3
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 3 ('Diagnostic skills and underlying knowledge base'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 4. Management and follow-up planning
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 4 ('Management and follow-up planning'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 4
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 4 ('Management and follow-up planning'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 5. Clinical judgement and decision making
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 5 ('Clinical judgement and decision making'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 5
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 5 ('Clinical judgement and decision making'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 6. Communication and listening skills
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 6 ('Communication and listening skills'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 6
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 6 ('Communication and listening skills'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 7. Organisation and time management
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 7 ('Organisation and time management'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 7
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 7 ('Organisation and time management'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: 8. Professionalism
fields.extractionHints: The ASSESSOR's rating for competency 8 ('Professionalism'). Set ONLY if their judgement for THIS item was specifically stated. Do NOT put a blanket 'everything else' rating here — that goes in 'Rating for all competencies'. Never guess. N/D/S/O.

fields.label: ↳ Comment on 8
fields.extractionHints: Any specific comment the assessor made about competency 8 ('Professionalism'). Record only what was said.

fields.label: Global summary

options.label: Level 0: Below that expected for early years training
Level 1: Appropriate for early years training
Level 2: Appropriate for completion of early years training or early specialty training
Level 3: Appropriate for central period of specialty training

fields.extractionHints: The assessor's overall level, if stated ('he said level 3'). Leave blank if not said.

fields.label: Rater feedback: general
fields.extractionHints: General feedback the assessor gave. Record ONLY what was said — never compose feedback.

fields.label: Rater feedback: strengths
fields.extractionHints: Strengths the assessor identified. Only what was said; blank if none stated.

fields.label: Rater feedback: development needs
fields.extractionHints: Development needs the assessor identified. Only what was said; blank if none stated.

fields.label: Rater feedback: recommended actions
fields.extractionHints: Actions the assessor recommended. Only what was said; blank if none stated.

fields.label: Trainee comments
fields.extractionHints: The trainee's own comments — your account, in your words.

fields.label: Reflection visibility
fields.extractionHints: Only set if the user says so.

fields.label: What did I learn from this experience?
fields.extractionHints:
The trainee's reflection answering: "What did I learn from this experience?"
This box should cover what the trainee LEARNT — the insight or knowledge they took from the case.

You are writing up the trainee's reflection so it reads as a strong, professional piece of reflective writing — the standard expected in a UK surgical training portfolio.

WRITE IT WELL. This is the main task.
• Use the GMC's reflective arc: WHAT happened that mattered · SO WHAT it meant (why it was significant, what you recognised) · WHAT NOW (what you take forward, what you will do differently).
• Focus on the LEARNING, not the story. GMC guidance is explicit: a reflective note should set out what you learnt and what you will do, not re-narrate the case. Keep case description to the minimum needed to make the learning make sense.
• Write in connected, considered prose — first person, past tense for events, professional register. Not bullet points, not tidied speech, not a transcript with the "um"s removed.
• Develop the trainee's thinking properly: draw out the insight they expressed, make its significance explicit, and state the consequence for their practice. Give each idea the sentences it deserves.
• Be specific. "I will use ultrasound guidance at first review" is reflection; "I will be more careful" is not.
• Structure is a scaffold, not a straitjacket — if the material doesn't fit the arc neatly, write it in whatever shape serves the thinking best.

THE ONE BOUNDARY — EVERY IDEA MUST BE THEIRS.
You are expressing the trainee's thinking fully and well. You are not supplying it. This note goes into a training record under their name and is read by an ARCP panel as evidence of THEIR reflective practice.
• Every learning point, insight, judgement, feeling and clinical fact must trace back to something the trainee actually said. Elaborate their idea; never introduce a new one.
• You may develop, sharpen and articulate a thought they expressed — that is the job. You may not conclude something they did not conclude.
• Do NOT add clinical facts, outcomes or complications they did not mention.
• Do NOT attribute feelings they did not express.
• Do NOT reach for stock reflective themes (human factors, situational awareness, team dynamics, "this highlighted the importance of communication") unless that is genuinely what they told you.
• If they said nothing that answers this question, return an EMPTY string. An empty box is honest; a fabricated reflection is not.

So: take what they gave you, however scrappy, and write the best, sharpest, most professional version of THAT.

Only answer THIS question here — do not duplicate the other reflection boxes.

fields.label: What did I do well?
fields.extractionHints:
The trainee's reflection answering: "What did I do well?"

fields.label: What do I need to improve or change? How will I achieve it?
fields.extractionHints:
The trainee's reflection answering: "What do I need to improve or change? How will I achieve it?"
This box should cover what the trainee needs to IMPROVE or change, and the concrete action they will take.
